section: AI Solutions Development Engineer (Generative AI & Automation)
text:
AI Solutions Development Engineer (Generative AI & Automation) 
A global organisation in the South West is expanding its internal Generative AI and automation 
capability and is hiring a Junior–Mid Level AI Solutions Development Engineer. 
This role is ideal for someone early in their career who is hands-on, adaptable, and motivated by 
building real AI solutions, with a strong focus on AI agents, automated workflows, and process 
automation rather than chatbot-only use cases.

section: automation rather than chatbot-only use cases.
text:
automation rather than chatbot-only use cases. 
You will join a small, high-impact AI solutions development team operating in an agile, lean 
innovation environment. The team works at the forefront of the Microsoft AI ecosystem, 
collaborating closely with Microsoft subject matter experts and selected third-party partners to take 
AI initiatives from idea through to scalable deployment. 
This is an excellent opportunity to gain exposure to cutting-edge generative AI capabilities, build

section: agent-enabled solutions that deliver measurable business value, and help shape h
text:
agent-enabled solutions that deliver measurable business value, and help shape how AI is adopted 
across a large enterprise. 
What We Offer: 
• 27 days annual leave (plus bank holidays), one well-being day, and three days of optional 
volunteer days for charity. 
• Hybrid working (two days in the office desired).  
• Private health care. 
• Minimum 8% and up to 10% pension contribution from the business. 
• World class training directly from Microsoft AI subject matter experts.

section: • Up to £45,000 per annum dependent on experience.
text:
• Up to £45,000 per annum dependent on experience.  
• Strong long-term career development within a growing AI solutions development function, 
with opportunities to grow technically or take on broader responsibility over time. 
Role Purpose 
To accelerate the organisation’s generative AI capability by designing, building, and delivering high-
impact AI solutions that solve real business problems. The role combines AI engineering, agent-based

section: workflow design, and practical automation, taking initiatives from early experim
text:
workflow design, and practical automation, taking initiatives from early experimentation through to 
validated and scalable implementation. 
Key Responsibilities: 
• Design, build, and deploy AI agent-enabled workflows that automate real business processes 
such as file creation, task execution, data movement, and database updates 
• Build agent-driven solutions using a combination of low-code, no-code, and pro-code 
approaches, selecting the most appropriate tools for each use case

section: • Develop and enhance RPA and automation solutions using Microsoft Power Platfor
text:
• Develop and enhance RPA and automation solutions using Microsoft Power Platform, 
including Power Automate and Copilot Studio 
• Identify when low-code tools reach their limits and transition solutions into more advanced, 
pro-code environments such as Azure AI Foundry, Fabric, and other Azure services 
• Work across the full solution lifecycle including use-case identification, experimentation, 
value assessment, design, build, validation, and deployment

section: • Collaborate closely with business stakeholders to understand requirements and
text:
• Collaborate closely with business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate 
them into practical, secure, and scalable AI solutions 
• Work with external development partners and specialist vendors where appropriate 
• Take ownership of delivery of at least one major AI solution, while supporting smaller 
initiatives as capacity allows 
• Support AI adoption across the organisation by demonstrating value, sharing knowledge, and 
helping others build confidence with generative AI

section: • Ensure solutions align with governance, security, compliance, and responsible
text:
• Ensure solutions align with governance, security, compliance, and responsible AI practices 
What We’re Looking For 
A motivated and adaptable engineer with a strong interest in generative AI, AI agents, and intelligent 
process automation. You enjoy building, experimenting, and iterating, and are comfortable working 
in environments where priorities evolve quickly. You are practical rather than theoretical and focused 
on delivering working solutions that create measurable value.

Ideal Background 
• Early-career to junior–mid level experience, typically one to three years, in AI, automation, 
software engineering, or a related field 
• Experience contributing to end-to-end solution delivery rather than research or 
experimentation alone 
• Hands-on experience building AI agents, agent-style workflows, automated business 
processes, or generative AI solutions beyond basic chatbots

section: • Strong interest in agentic systems that interact with processes, APIs, data so
text:
• Strong interest in agentic systems that interact with processes, APIs, data sources, and 
business workflows 
• Familiarity with the Microsoft technology stack, ideally including Power Platform, Power 
Automate, Copilot Studio, and Azure AI services such as Foundry or Fabric 
• Comfortable working in small, agile teams and taking ownership of delivery 
• Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly to non-
technical stakeholders

technical stakeholders  
Beneficial (not required) 
• Python or other scripting or backend development languages 
• Experience with generative AI or agent frameworks such as LangChain, Semantic Kernel, or 
similar tools 
• Git, GitHub, and basic CI/CD concepts 
• Exposure to non-Microsoft AI platforms such as OpenAI, Anthropic, AWS, or Google 
• Experience working with or alongside third-party development partners
